rows.notes: Top-of-page title clearly written and centered: 'Receipts Anno presentis.' very legible.

rows.description: from ye Annuity out of ye Exchequer
rows.notes: Clear hand and well aligned with leftmost of the three vertical ruling lines; reads as £50 0s 0d unambiguously.

rows.description: from Mr Wells 1/2 rent due at xmas 1743
rows.notes: Amount reads clearly as '01 00 00' aligned under pounds column. Description is legible; minor uncertainty only in punctuation of phrase.

rows.description: from Mrs Rougier Earnest for house at Buckgate
rows.notes: Amount aligned and reads as '00 01 00' (1 shilling). Surname in description is somewhat unclear (reads 'Rougier'/'Roupier'); chosen reading based on letter shapes.
Numerals '46 03 04' are well-formed and aligned with the pounds/shillings/pence lines. Description is clearly legible.
Amount '10 00 00' is clear. The name in the description is faint/irregular (read as 'Minchennitt' or similar); the reading is plausible but not certain.

rows.description: from Mrs Shiers Augm. to repay ye Deo. of Money out of ye Chest to pay ye X mas
rows.notes: Amount reads as '50 00 00' clearly. Description is long but readable; a few abbreviations ('Deo.' / 'X mas') are interpreted as they appear.

rows.description: from Mrs Jennings to ye Rent at Day 1744
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings read clearly as '22 02'. Pence column shows '1' with a small trailing loop/mark interpreted as '3q' (three farthings = 0.75). The fraction mark is faint but consistent with 3q.

rows.description: from Mr Hawtin South Newinton ½ yr pd
rows.notes: Amount '28 00 00' is clear. The description has an abbreviation at the end (read as '½ yr pd' or similar); the place-name 'South Newinton' is somewhat cramped but legible.

rows.description: from Mr Lord ½ yr & Day 1744
rows.notes: Pounds/shillings/pence read as '01 15 08' and there is a clear small loop after the pence interpreted as halfpenny (0.5). Description contains abbreviations; overall moderately clear.

rows.description: from Mrs Roupier ¼ at Mie
rows.notes: Amount '01 02 06' is clean and aligned. Surname spelling matches the earlier Roupier/Rougier entry; the place 'Mie' is somewhat abbreviated but visible.

A subtotal number is present at this location reading '210 05 02½'. The hundreds digit (2) and tens (1) are legible but a faint possibility exists it could be '240'; chosen as '210' based on the visible pen strokes. Fraction is a halfpenny (loop).
Section header centered on the page and clearly written as 'Gent. Commoners'.
Amount '08 00 00' clearly aligned. Name 'Georgius Ashby' legible in the hand used.
Amount reads '08 00 00'. Surname spelled 'Hammersley' (handwriting is clear).
Section header clearly written 'Commoners Library'.

rows.description: from Mr Webber Jun. for xmas 1735. 1739. 1740. 1741. 1742
rows.notes: Amount '06 10 00' clear. The list of years in the description is legible though compact; 'Webber Jun.' is read as written.
Small centered header 'Act Supper.' is clearly visible.
Amount '03 00 00' clearly written and aligned; name 'Dr Andrew' legible.

A small standalone amount '25 10 00' appears in the right column with a preceding 'L' mark. No label on the left. The digits are legible but context is unclear (intermediate calculation), so confidence is moderate.

Another stacked total reads '240 05 02½' (halfpenny tail visible). The hundreds/tens digits are a little faint (could be 210 in a different reading) but here read as '240' given pen thickness; marked as low-medium confidence.

Third figure in the small stacked column appears as '409 10 02' (the final digits are slightly cramped and faint). Context suggests an accumulated subtotal; reading is less certain than other numeric rows.
Label 'Sum. on Receipts' is clear. Amount reads '545 05 10' cleanly and aligns with the receipts column.
Text 'from Ball. of last Acct.' is legible and amount '93 16 11' is clear. All currency fields fall within valid ranges.
Final total line 'Sum. tot. £' with amount '739 02 09' is clearly written and well aligned; high confidence in reading.
